mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.02 over 3-year rolling windows or positive return frequency drops below 55% during rate cut periods.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.4 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations and trigger negative S&P 500 returns within 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of triggered instances or signal loses predictive power when baseline unemployment falls below 5.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous monthly increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer and business demand expansion, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive MoM signals yield negative 60-day returns in >50% of cases or the interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the industrial production index during periods of high inflation indicates supply-side bottlenecks that compress corporate margins and cause immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Signal fails to predict negative returns when CPIAUCSL MoM growth exceeds 0.5% or 5-day return distribution shows no significant left tail skew.

mechanism_narrative: Falling real interest rates, calculated as the federal funds rate minus core inflation, reduce equity valuation discount factors and generate positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Real rate declines coincide with negative 20-day returns in >55% of observations or the interaction effect vanishes when inflation expectations are anchored.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands aggregate income and corporate revenue forecasts, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Payroll growth series shows zero correlation with 60-day forward returns or Rank IC falls below 0.01 across all rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly real GDP contraction exceeding 0.5% triggers recession fears that immediately depress equity valuations, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns within the following week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 posts positive 5-day returns in >65% of contraction quarters or the signal loses predictive accuracy when accompanied by aggressive central bank intervention.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during a post-recession recovery phase signals expanding manufacturing output and rising corporate earnings, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Industrial production acceleration fails to predict positive 20-day returns when capacity utilization exceeds 85% or signal IC turns negative in out-of-sample tests.
